Terms of Service

Effective Date: September 25, 2025

Contents 1. Purpose of Site 2. Acceptable Use 3. Intellectual Property 4. Disclaimers 5. Limitation of Liability 6. Third-Party Links 7. Changes to the Terms 8. Governing Law 9. Contact

1. Purpose of Site

zrlo.dev (the “Site”) is a personal portfolio and demonstration website operated by Zachary Ryan Lopez. The Site is provided for informational purposes to showcase professional experience, projects, and writing samples.

2. Acceptable Use

  • Do not use the Site for unlawful, harmful, or fraudulent activities.
  • Do not attempt to disrupt, overload, or compromise the Site’s security or availability.
  • Do not copy, redistribute, or misrepresent Site content without prior permission, unless a page explicitly provides a license.

3. Intellectual Property

Unless otherwise noted, all original content on the Site (including text, design, graphics, and code) is the intellectual property of Zachary Ryan Lopez. Third‑party trademarks, logos, and links remain the property of their respective owners.

Code snippets or repositories linked from the Site may be governed by separate licenses in their respective repositories.

4. Disclaimers

The Site is provided “as is” and “as available,” without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to fitness for a particular purpose, merchantability, and non‑infringement. We do not warrant that the Site will be error‑free or uninterrupted.

5. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, zrlo.dev and its owner will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for any loss of profits, data, or use, arising out of or related to your use of the Site.

6. Third‑Party Links

For convenience, the Site may link to third‑party websites (such as GitHub or LinkedIn). We do not control and are not responsible for the content, policies, or practices of those sites.

7. Changes to the Terms

We may modify these Terms from time to time. Changes are effective when posted on the Site as indicated by the “Effective Date” above. Your continued use of the Site constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

8. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Texas and applicable U.S. federal law, without regard to conflict‑of‑law principles.

9. Contact

For questions about these Terms, email terms@zrlo.dev.
